mnemonics: DETA vs NETA: Deterministic is like a single path, Nondeterministic is like multiple paths.
Q: What does epsilon mean? A: Epsilon is like a wildcard in automata.
Search for a word: Start at the beginning, move forward one step at a time, epsilon-transitions are like skipping ahead.
keyPoints: Definition of Deterministic Finite Automata (DFA): A 7-tuple (Q, Σ, δ, q0, F, Q0, Γ) where Q is the set of states, Σ is the alphabet, δ is the transition function, q0 is the initial state, F is the set of final states, Q0 is the set of initial states, and Γ is the set of stack symbols.

Definition of Nondeterministic Finite Automata (NFA): A 7-tuple (Q, Σ, δ, q0, F, Q0, Γ) where Q is the set of states, Σ is the alphabet, δ is the transition function, q0 is the initial state, F is the set of final states, Q0 is the set of initial states, and Γ is the set of stack symbols.
Text Search using DFA: Preprocess the text to create a DFA, then search for the pattern by starting at the beginning and checking each state.
Text Search using NFA: Preprocess the text to create an NFA, then search for the pattern by starting at the beginning and checking each state.
Epsilon-Transitions: Allow the automaton to move from a state to another state without consuming an input symbol.
